differences.title: 1. SECURITY
differences.leftDesc: Node.js allows broad access to file system and network by default.
differences.rightDesc: Deno requires explicit permission for file system and network access.

differences.title: 2. MODULE SYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Node.js uses CommonJS modules, requiring `require()` and `module.exports`.
differences.rightDesc: Deno natively supports ES Modules with `import`/`export` syntax.

differences.title: 3. STANDARD LIBRARY
differences.leftDesc: Node.js relies heavily on third-party packages for many utilities.
differences.rightDesc: Deno provides built-in modules for tasks like HTTP servers and file I/O.

differences.title: 4. PACKAGE MANAGEMENT
differences.leftDesc: Node.js uses `npm` or `yarn` with `package.json` for dependencies.
differences.rightDesc: Deno imports modules directly from URLs, simplifying dependency management.

differences.title: 5. TYPESCRIPT SUPPORT
differences.leftDesc: Node.js requires a separate compilation step for TypeScript.
differences.rightDesc: Deno has built-in TypeScript support, no extra setup needed.

differences.title: 6. API DESIGN
differences.leftDesc: Node.js historically used callbacks extensively, now with Promises and async/await.
differences.rightDesc: Deno is built around Promises and async/await from the ground up.

differences.title: 7. WEBSOCKETS
differences.leftDesc: Node.js requires libraries like `ws` for WebSocket functionality.
differences.rightDesc: Deno provides native support for WebSocket clients and servers.
